section: 4 Best practices to scale your startup
text: We know that getting your startup off the ground and flying takes a lot of time and effort. There are no substitutes or magic solutions to realising your ambitions other the hard work of the founders and first employees, but you can follow a series of good practices that can get yours there faster and in a scalable and repeatable way . Here are 4 best practices to scale your startup fast: Get the foundation right The startup environment is a fast moving and dynamic world where things change quickly and growing companies can get left behind. Establishing a good foundation will make your company more stable as it grows. In such a dynamic environment, startups tend to focus all their efforts on getting their product to market as soon as possible, and key elements for the development of the company such as company policies or legal agreements are often given lower priority.  But good governance from the outset is an investment that will establish a stable foundation and pay off and in the medium to long term. Comprehensive company policies are not only key to the development of the company as staff know what is expected of them, it also sends a clear message to your (potential) customers that you take governance and good practice seriously. Here are 5 key areas: 1) InfoSec (Information Security) – These policies are designed to protect your business, intellectual property and staff. Their goal is to display how your company is always ready, protected and up to date with the latest security news and threats. Examples of InfoSec policies include: Access Control policy, Cloud Services policy, Remote Access policy or InfoSec Management policy. 2) Staff – Team or staff policies aim to build, inform and protect your team. They will establish the mechanisms to bring new members to your team, as well as the conditions in which they will work for your company. In the post-covid world, these policies are becoming increasingly important due to the increase in remote working. Paternity/Maternity Leave policy, Flexible working policy, Health and Safety policy, Holiday policy and Remuneration policy are the most usual examples of staff policies . 3) Compliance – The main goal of governance or compliance policies is to show potential prospects that your company is ready and safe to do business with. Examples of compliance policies are: Data Protection policy, Modern Slavery Statement or Complaints policy. 4) ESG – Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) policies outline your company’s commitment to society and the environment. They are designed to manage your resources and the environmental impacts of your business, achieve cost savings and promote more environmentally friendly practices. These include: Environmental policy, and Corporate and Social Responsibility policy. Close deals with well written agreements Once the company has a good state of basic governance, the next step is to ensure water tight agreements are in place with your customers, suppliers, collaborators, employees, consultants… Creating your own library of legal agreements gives you the flexibility to close deals with different stakeholders in record time. Well written agreements help you sell your services, build your team both in-house and outsourced, and protect your business and intellectual property. Adoptech makes creating agreements easy.  Written by lawyers, our agreements are a must-have for a growing startup and include: Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA), Professional Services contracts, Data processing agreements and SaaS MSA Terms and Conditions. Get ISO Certified Once your startup has established a good customer base, a market-fit product and a stable foundation, the next natural step for its growth is the Enterprise world . Enterprise deals are difficult to achieve and close as they require a high level of compliance, precise infosecurity hygiene and specific requirements depending on each company. In the case of startups, perhaps the best way to approach this process is through certification of a recognised international standard that confirms their level of compliance. ISO Certification is the most established and recognised standard, covering many different fields and certifications. ISO Certification processes are considered complex and time consuming , they require a high level of commitment from the company and its employees. The most efficient way to achieve ISO certification is to use experienced consultants who know their way around the certification process and can help your company hit the ground running, avoiding unnecessary delays and costly mistakes. At Adoptech we have done the work to make ISO 27001 certification simple and cost effective , if you want more information on how we can help your company, click here ! Manage and reduce your risks Establishing your company and signing contracts with large customers is hard work, but when you reach this point, it is more important than ever to maintain a high level of information security to keep those customers . Security, data protection and cybersecurity breaches are unfortunately becoming more and more frequent in the online world and the trends of the post-covid world are intensifying this. Companies, especially startups, must have processes in place to identify and manage them quickly so they can minimise their impac t. Processes around BCP and DR , managing data breaches and ensuring due diligence is undertaken to efficiently manage the risks associated with suppliers to prevent a disruption in your supply chain, are key to keeping your startup running should the worst happen. The Adoptech platform is the one-stop place for all the tools to start, grow and scale a Startup. It provides configurable statutory, InfoSec , ESG , Staff and Compliance policies with a library of over 70 policies . The Adoptech platform aims to start your company on the right path from the beginning, creating a single source of truth in your enterprise data room and immediately preventing bad practice, productivity sapping use of spreadsheets and email workflows that cannot be scaled or repeated. Adoptech enables companies to create their own BCP DR and manage all their vendors and their risks associated in the same place. If you want to see how Adoptech can help your Startup, contact us here ! Al Goodwin Back to News

section: 5 Policies to keep your startup up to date with the 2022 trends
text: The 2019-2022 period has brought n ew trends that have disrupted traditional standards in several fields. Events with global repercussions in recent years have directly affected not only the work situation, but also the personal situation of millions of people around the world. The pandemic has changed the traditional view of work , introducing new concepts that are here to stay. As a company, it is very important that corporate policies reflect this new situation and protect both employees and the company itself. In this post we will talk about 5 policies to keep your startup up to date with the trends of 2022. Home Working Policy Also know as: Remote Working Policy Remote working is here to stay. All companies have embraced this trend, adapting to their specific situation. Offering the flexibility of home working to your employees can offer a number of benefits to both parties, increasing morale, improving staff retention and even saving on office space and recruitment. However, a policy to regulate this situation is becoming increasingly necessary . This policy should operates on the principle that any request to work from home will only be approved if it is mutually beneficial. This policy should be on the same page as other similar policies, such as the flexible working policy, to ensure uniformity of criteria. Learn more about the Home Working policy here VPN (Virtual Private Network) Policy The growth of remote working has led to an increase in potentially dangerous situations online. The concept of VPN was practically unknown a few years ago, and today it has become a must for many companies and workers. VPNs guarantee private and secure internet Access , providing an extra level of security to your Company and employees. The Virtual Private Network Policy should provide guidance on how VPN connections can be made and how that access should be controlled. Learn more about the VPN policy here Mobile Device Policy (BYOD) Also known as: Bring Your Own Device Policy The line between personal and professional environments is becoming increasingly thinner. The use of personal devices in the professional environment and vice versa is becoming more frequent. The mobile phone policy is used to communicate acceptable use specific to portable devices, such as mobile phones. The use of mobile devices can present a security and compliance risk to companies, it is therefore prudent to put in place a specific policy that goes into further detail than the Acceptable Use policy that provides broader guidance on the appropriate use of business and personal equipment. Employees are very likely to use personal mobile phones during working hours, as a result, most firms allow a reasonable level of use, but being consistent in defining and enforcing rules is recommended to ensure that use does not become excessive. Learn more about the Mobile Device policy here Internet and Social Media Policy Social networks are the meeting point between company, audience and workers, making them a v ery delicate place for the image and reputation of the brand . Any communication by a member of the staff whether on behalf of the Company or in a personal capacity can significantly impact the Company’s reputation. The Internet and Social Media Policy aims to ensure that all staff are aware of their responsibilities and obligations with regard to using the internet and social media. The policy outlines the C ompany’s position on the use of social media and networking sites, and all types of electronic message/blogging by staff whilst at work, and/or representing the Company. Learn more about the Internet and Social Media policy here Unlimited Holiday Policy The progressive increase in labor flexibility has directly affected holidays management. Even though an Unlimited Holiday policy is still the exception rather than the norm, this trend keeps growing, and it is particularly prevalent in SaaS companies. This flexibility may attract highly qualified candidates who require the flexibility of an unlimited holiday policy, and it is being tested as a way to encourage a more efficient use of work time. However, this flexibility may have potential drawbacks, since it will not work for every type of worker and/or company. Workers whose jobs require on-site presence could be an example of this. In any case, it is necessary for the company to be clear about the characteristics and limitations of this practice , through an Unlimited Holiday Policy. Learn more about the Unlimited Holiday policy here Corporate policies must always be up to date, especially in today’s dynamic and changing environment. New trends, with their advantages, disadvantages and risks for the company and workers continue to emerge, in addition to legal requirements. Creating new policies, updating your current ones, have them signed by the executives, sharing them with your employees… all the process is time consuming and takes time from your real core business. With Adoptech , you can manage more than 50 policies in the same platform, sign and share them in seconds, track staff approval and get automated updates to make the whole process more efficient. Start for free here or chat with our team if you have any questions. Al Goodwin Back to News

text: Right now all your energy is consumed on building your service, MVP’s, winning pilots, initial sales and building towards your next investment round. Getting your software start-up ready is hard enough, the last thing you need is to spend time writing company policies, right? Wrong. Prospective clients and investors don’t care that you are a startup or that you are all working flat out and wearing various hats, they are still going to require evidence that your company is being governed in a way that protects their data and reputation. Policies are also a great way for the founders to communicate to their team about the culture, frameworks and expectations they have for success. Teams work better when an operating framework is defined. Spoken instructions are never enough. When everyone involved is working hard to get things off the ground, establishing clear ground rules will help your staff understand how to operate and to avoid conflict or dispute; then if you can’t avoid legal dispute, a well-written policy will limit any damage. … so where to start. There is no magic formula on how to start a software company, but we can help you with the 6 essential policies for software start-ups we advise you put in place from Day 1. Health and Safety Policy UK law states that every business must have a policy for managing health and safety. It’s imperative that your staff work within a healthy and safe workplace. If you have fewer than five employees you don’t have to write down your occupational health policy but it’s good practice and we’ve made it easy. Accidents and unsafe conditions can land you in court. You can learn more about the Health and Safety policy here . Home and Remote Working Policy The workplace for most software companies, at least at the outset of operations, is the founder’s home or a garage, but health and safety obligations still apply, so we advise putting in place a home working policy from the outset. The policy outlines home working arrangements, equipment and safety for remote working. Longer-term, offering the flexibility of home or remote working can help to increase staff morale and improve staff retention. Software companies being open to remote working can also unlock access to a large pool of offshore talent that may be hard to find locally at a cost-effective price point. The home working policy compliments the health and safety policy. You can read more about it here . Equal Opportunity, Diversity and Inclusion Policy There is no specific legal requirement in the UK to provide a written equal opportunities policy at work. Nonetheless, under the UK’s Equality Act 2010, it is unlawful to discriminate against someone in the workplace and setting out your company’s commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion from an early stage makes it clear that equal opportunities are a reality at your company. You can learn more about the Equal Opportunity, Diversity and Inclusion policy here . Website Privacy and Cookie Policy Key to marketing your software services is your website and it’s often one of the first things you will develop. It is important that you outline the procedures your company has in place to protect website visitors’ personal data.  Your policy should be aligned with data protection regulation(s) and inform visitors of the type of data you may collect, how you might use it and the processes in place to protect that data. As well as reassuring website visitors, it also ensures that you have their permission to store cookies on their computer. The policy is aligned with UK and EU data privacy laws. You can read more about the Website Privacy and Cookies policy here . Employee Grievance and Disciplinary Policy By UK law, all employers must set out a grievance procedure and share it in writing with all employees. Demonstrate that you are a responsible employer, meet your legal obligations and ensure problems, complaints or concerns that employees may have been dealt with fairly, consistently, and in a timely fashion. Occasionally problems will arise at work and dealing with them is much easier with a clear disciplinary policy in place. Employees must understand how and under what circumstances, they will be disciplined. A standardised step-by-step process will help you ensure fair and appropriate treatment, even if you don’t formally disclose the entire procedure. It will also show that you are an employer who does not tolerate serious violations but also values remedial actions in the case of minor offences. Providing procedures to ensure employees are treated fairly and that your company is less likely to encounter expensive, time-consuming tribunal claims. You can read more about the Grievance policy here . Open Source Software Policy You’ve started development and want to reach MVP as soon as possible. Like most companies, you take advantage of the Open-source software (OSS) available to reduce development costs and in some cases improve the reliability of the software being developed. Are all your team aware of the risks that using externally developed OSS may present to your company? The last thing most software companies would wish for is to find out their codebase is reliant on OSS that has a restrictive licence, requiring it to be made freely available. OSS licences differ but it is key that all those involved in the development and approval of its usefully understand the implications. This policy aims to educate the team on the risks and sets out the processes that should be followed before any OSS is utilised. You can read more about the Open Source Software Policy here . Getting started is a challenge and that’s why we’ve made it simple. Don’t waste time downloading templates, editing them, emailing them back and forth. Adoptech allows you to quickly build, maintain and share your company policies so that you can start your new business on the front foot. Our policy generator has over 70 policies specially designed for software companies. Get started here or chat with one of the team if you have any questions. Al Goodwin Back to News

section: Adoptech CAF Assurance and Trustmark for MSPs
text: Overview With the UK Cyber Security and Resilience Bill coming into force, Managed Service Providers (MSPs) will soon fall under the Network and Information Systems (NIS) Regulations . The NCSC’s Cyber Assessment Framework (CAF) will form the basis for assessing MSP compliance. Adoptech’s CAF Assurance Scheme helps MSPs prepare for, evidence, and independently validate compliance with the CAF principles. Achieving Adoptech CAF Assurance provides trusted, third-party assurance that your organisation has provided evidence of compliance with the CAF controls you have in scope. How the Assurance Process Works 1. Onboard and Configure Begin by onboarding to the Adoptech automated compliance platform , where the full CAF framework is available in a simple, guided format. You’ll: Upload or generate policies using the Adoptech policy wizard Complete the Risk , Supplier and Legal Registers Assign owners for controls, policies, and KPIs Upload evidence manually or automatically through integrations Each CAF control includes plain English guidance and example treatment plans to help you define your internal processes. You can also tailor the scope of controls to fit your organisation’s specific risks and operations. 2. Prepare for Assessment Next, you’ll review each CAF control and confirm it meets the “Achieved” status. You can upload supporting documentation or configure integrations (e.g. AWS, Microsoft 365, GitHub) to automatically collect evidence of compliance. Your organisation’s Trust Centre page can also be set up to demonstrate transparency to clients — showing your real-time control status, subprocessors, and incident updates, supporting CAF’s good practice principles for communication and assurance. 3. Independent Assessment Once your preparation is complete, an independent Adoptech assessor will review your CAF framework. This will be carried out on an annual basis. They’ll: Evaluate the evidence you’ve provided against your control set Generate a summary report detailing the controls reviewed 4. Accreditation and Publication If the evidence demonstrates you are meeting the CAF controls in scope your organisation will be awarded Adoptech CAF Assured Trustmark . You’ll receive: An independent audit report An Trustmark Badge for use on your Trust Centre  and marketing materials You can also choose to publish your accreditation summary on your Trust Centre page , alongside your governance documents and real-time compliance status, to give customers and regulators visibility of your cyber assurance posture. Why It Matters Meet NIS and ICO regulatory expectations Demonstrate cyber maturity and transparency Identify and close compliance gaps early Gain independent, third-party validation Get Started Start building confidence in your security posture — and prepare for NIS readiness — with Adoptech CAF Assurance . Our regulatory consultants are also available to provide further NIS guidance, run programmes and conduct internal audits. Please contact a member of the team and ask to speak to our Professional Services Team. Adoptech is providing this service for MSPs in partnership with Brigantia. Book a Consultation with Brigantia Start Readiness Assessment Al Goodwin Back to News

text: Last update: October 18, 2024 Reporting Security Vulnerabilities to Adoptech At Adoptech, safeguarding our services and ensuring data security is our top priority. If you’re a security researcher who has identified a security flaw in our Services, we appreciate your assistance in responsibly disclosing it to us. Our responsible disclosure process is managed through HackerOne’s Vulnerability Disclosure Program (VDP) . To report a vulnerability, please use the submission form at the bottom of this page. Please note that only vulnerabilities submitted via HackerOne’s program will be considered for a reward. If you’ve previously responsibly disclosed a vulnerability to us, we extend our gratitude to you. When reporting a vulnerability, please provide a detailed description of the attack scenario, exploitability level, impact on Adoptech and/or its customers, and a comprehensive report with reproducible steps. Incomplete reports that do not allow us to replicate the issue will not be eligible for a reward. The following are the HackerOne program rules for responsible disclosure: Accessing any customer data is always strictly prohibited. Accessing any Adoptech internal data is always strictly prohibited. Submit only one vulnerability at a time unless vulnerabilities are chained together to demonstrate impact. When duplicate submissions occur, we award only the first reproducible report received. Multiple vulnerabilities having a single underlying root cause will be awarded singularly. Social engineering (e.g. phishing, vishing, smishing) is prohibited. Privacy violations, destruction of data, and interruption of degradation of our service must be avoided. You must only use accounts you own or have the explicit permission of the account owner. Results matching findings from SSL/TLS testing sites, Security Score sites, or similar will not be eligible for bounty. Exclusions Known vulnerabilities are eligible for reward and may be marked as duplicates if the root cause aligns too closely with an already reported issue. The following issues are considered out of scope: Any activity that could lead to the disruption of our service (DoS). Clickjacking on pages with no sensitive actions. Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) on unauthenticated forms or forms with no sensitive actions Previously known vulnerable libraries without a working Proof of Concept. Content spoofing and text injection issues without showing an attack vector/without being able to modify HTML/CSS. Missing best practices in Content Security Policy. Missing email best practices (for example, invalid, incomplete or missing SPF/DKIM/DMARC records). Vulnerabilities affecting users of outdated or unpatched browsers. Public Zero-day vulnerabilities that have had an official patch available for less than 1 month will be awarded on a case-by-case basis. Open redirect (without additional security impact demonstrated). Contact If you have any questions or suggestions feel free to contact us at [email protected]
page_title: Adoptech Responsible Disclosure - Adoptech

section: Adoptech vs ISMS.online: Complete Compliance Platform Comparison
text: Navigating the compliance platform landscape can be challenging, especially when evaluating established solutions like ISMS.online against newer alternatives. We’ve created this comprehensive analysis to compare Adoptech and ISMS.online across features, automation capabilities, pricing models, and organisational fit, helping you understand which platform might better serve your compliance requirements. The Evolution of Compliance Management The compliance landscape has transformed dramatically in recent years. Organisations no longer view security compliance as a necessary burden but as a competitive advantage that builds customer trust and enables business growth. With 83% of organisations experiencing more than one data breach during 2022 and regulatory fines reaching unprecedented levels, choosing the right compliance platform has become a strategic business decision. Two platforms frequently emerge in discussions about ISO 27001 and multi-framework compliance: ISMS.online and Adoptech. Both serve organisations across various industries, but their approaches to automation, user experience, and customer success differ significantly. Understanding these differences is crucial for making an informed decision that aligns with your organisation’s needs, resources, and growth trajectory. This detailed comparison examines both platforms across all critical dimensions to help you determine which solution best serves your compliance objectives. Platform Foundations: Understanding Each Approach ISMS.online: The Established Solution for Consultants ISMS.online has built its reputation as a comprehensive compliance platform with particular strength in ISO 27001 certification. Launched with a focus on providing detailed documentation and audit preparation tools, the platform has expanded to support multiple frameworks including GDPR, SOC 2, and various data protection standards. Core Philosophy : ISMS.online operates on the principle that compliance requires comprehensive documentation and detailed process management. The platform provides extensive templates, document libraries, and workflow structures designed to guide organisations and their consultants through complex certification processes. Market Position : Positioned as an enterprise-grade solution, ISMS.online typically serves larger organisations with dedicated compliance teams who can navigate its comprehensive feature set and invest time in thorough implementation. Key Characteristics : Comprehensive document management system Detailed risk assessment frameworks Structured audit preparation tools Extensive policy template libraries Multi-framework support capabilities Adoptech: The Intelligent Automation Platform Adoptech represents a new generation of compliance platforms that combine advanced automation with expert human guidance. Built with the understanding that modern businesses need efficiency without sacrificing thoroughness, Adoptech automates up to 80% of compliance tasks while maintaining the depth required for successful certification. Core Philosophy : Adoptech believes compliance should be accessible, efficient, and supported by genuine expertise. The platform combines intelligent automation with dedicated UK-based compliance specialists to deliver both technological efficiency and human insight. Market Position : Designed to serve organisations of all sizes, from growing SMEs to established enterprises, Adoptech positions itself as the platform that makes enterprise-grade compliance accessible and affordable. Key Characteristics : Automated evidence collection and monitoring Integrated expert consultancy services Flexible pricing models Modern SaaS tool integrations Streamlined user experience with guided workflows Comprehensive Feature Analysis Compliance Framework Support ISMS.online : ISO 27001 (primary specialisation), GDPR and data protection frameworks, SOC 2 Type I and Type II, ISO 9001 quality management, and custom framework development capabilities. The platform’s support is comprehensive but requires significant setup and configuration for each standard. Adoptech : ISO 27001 with automated gap analysis, SOC 2 with continuous monitoring, GDPR with data mapping automation, DORA (Digital Operational Resilience Act), Cyber Essentials, Cyber Essentials Plus, CAF, NIS2  and many more integrated frameworks. Adoptech’s approach emphasises seamless integration between 15+ frameworks, reducing duplication of effort and cross-referencing automatically. Analysis : While both support major frameworks, Adoptech’s integrated approach reduces duplication of effort and provides better visibility. ISMS.online offers more granular control but requires greater resource investment. Automation and Efficiency Capabilities ISMS.online: Automated reminder systems, scheduled risk assessment notifications, basic document version control, automated audit trail generation, and template-based process workflows. ISMS.online’s automation primarily focuses on administrative tasks and notifications, relying heavily on manual input and review processes. Adoptech: This is where Adoptech shines. It offers: a utomated evidence collection from integrated systems, real-time compliance monitoring, intelligent risk assessment with automated scoring, automated policy generation and updates, continuous security control testing, and smart integration with 50+ SaaS tools. Adoptech automates up to 90% of routine compliance tasks, maintaining continuous compliance without constant manual intervention. Analysis: The automation gap is substantial. Adoptech’s comprehensive automation significantly reduces the ongoing effort required to maintain compliance, making it invaluable for resource-constrained organisations. User Experience (UX) and Interface Design ISMS.online: Comprehensive dashboard with detailed information density, hierarchical navigation structure, document-centric workflow design, and a traditional enterprise software interface. The platform has a steep learning curve for new users and requires investment in training to achieve proficiency. Adoptech: Clean, intuitive dashboard design, guided workflows for common tasks, visual progress tracking and compliance status, and a rapid user onboarding process. Adoptech prioritises user adoption and efficiency, guiding users through complex compliance processes without overwhelming them. Analysis: For organisations prioritising quick adoption and user satisfaction, Adoptech’s streamlined approach offers significant advantages. ISMS.online’s comprehensive interface serves organisations with dedicated compliance specialists who can fully utilise its extensive capabilities. Integration and Connectivity Capabilities ISMS.online: Basic API connectivity, manual data import/export functions, email notification systems, standard reporting interfaces and limited third-party tool integrations. This approach focuses on core business systems and document management, requiring manual processes for most data collection and evidence gathering. Adoptech: 50+ pre-built SaaS tool integrations, cloud infrastructure monitoring (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud), security tool connections (vulnerability scanners, SIEM systems)  identity provider integrations (Okta, etc.), and automated evidence collection pipelines. Adoptech’s extensive library enables automated evidence collection and real-time compliance monitoring, significantly reducing manual effort. Analysis: Adoptech’s comprehensive connectivity enables true automation. ISMS.online’s limited integrations require substantial manual effort. Detailed Pricing Analysis ISMS.online Pricing Structure ISMS.online does not publish pricing plans but based on market research and user reports, pricing typically follows this structure: Estimated Pricing Ranges : Small organisation (1-50 employees): £3,000-£8,000 annually Medium organisation (51-200 employees): £8,000-£20,000 annually Large organisation (200+ employees): £20,000-£50,000+ annually Enterprise solutions: Custom pricing, often £50,000+ annually Additional Costs : Implementation and setup fees, training and onboarding costs, premium support packages, and additional framework licenses. Adoptech Pricing Structure Adoptech focuses on providing value-based pricing, aligning costs with the complexity and size of your organisation and ensuring enterprise-grade compliance is accessible and affordable. Platform Starting Cost: Adoptech’s pricing starts at £250 per month. Pricing Drivers: The cost scales efficiently based on the size of your company and the number of compliance frameworks required. This ensures you only pay for the compliance scope you need. ISO 27001: Pricing for this foundational certification starts at £400 per month. This includes the full platform automation. Dedicated expert guidance, which drastically reduces your internal resource overhead is also available. Specialised Discounts: Adoptech is committed to making compliance accessible to organisations with limited resources: Startups are eligible for discounts. Registered Charities receive significant discounts to support their critical work. Included Value : Adoptech’s pricing is all-inclusive, including project management,  security awareness training, phishing simulations, device vulnerability scanning and the ability to publish your own Trust Centre. Implementation and Support Comparison ISMS.online Implementation Process Typical Timeline : 3-6 months for full implementation. Implementation Approach : Comprehensive initial assessment, detailed platform configuration, extensive user training requirements, process workflow setup, integration configuration (limited) and document template customisation. Resource Requirements : Dedicated project manager recommended, technical resources for setup, and ongoing administrative oversight. Implementation services are charged separately. Adoptech Implementation Process Typical Timeline : 8-12 week standard implementation. Implementation Approach : Automated platform setup, guided onboarding with a compliance specialist, pre-configured integration setup, and immediate access to automation features. Support Structure : Dedicated UK-based compliance specialists provide project management, weekly stand-ups, consultancy and guidance, and implementation guidance included in all plans. Resource Requirements : Minimal internal project management needed, simplified user adoption, and automated evidence collection reduces ongoing effort. Analysis: Adoptech’s streamlined implementation enables faster time-to-value while maintaining thoroughness through automation and expert guidance. Customer Success and Outcomes The table below provides a comparison based on available information and user feedback. Metric ISMS.online Success Metrics Adoptech Success Metrics Certification Pass Rate Industry-standard pass rates 100% certification pass rate Administrative Effort Requires significant learning investment and manual effort Average 80% reduction in compliance administrative tasks Implementation Time 3-6 months 8-12 week implementation timelines Best For Large enterprise environments with dedicated teams Organisations of all sizes seeking efficiency and time savings Risk Management and Security Capabilities Risk Management Approach ISMS.online : Comprehensive risk register and detailed risk scoring methodologies, but relies on manual risk assessment workflows and traditional approaches. Limited real-time monitoring capabilities. Adoptech : As a GRC platform Adoptech provides comprehensive risk registers that cover multiple categories of risk, including operational, systemic, technical, AI-related, environmental and many more. Users can review and select relevant risks from the embedded Risk Library, with each risk automatically mapped to the appropriate controls. As control statuses are updated through automated testing, organisations gain immediate visibility into their current risk exposure and the effectiveness of their controls. Adoptech’s modern approach delivers efficient, streamlined risk management with real-time clarity. Vendor and Third-Party Risk Management ISMS.online : Basic vendor assessment templates and manual vendor risk evaluation processes. Limited automation in vendor monitoring. Adoptech : A vendor register is provided to track and assess risk. Vendor contractual agreements can be linked and contracts can be generated within Adoptech. The platform supports the ability to send third-party risk assessments and track responses. Clients can utilise the questionnaire templates provided or upload their own. Scalability and Growth Considerations ISMS.online Scalability : Suitable for large, established organisations, but requires proportional resource scaling. Manual processes limit efficiency at scale. Adoptech Scalability : Cloud-native platform designed for elastic scaling. Automation maintains efficiency regardless of size. Resource requirements remain minimal as organisations grow. Adoptech’s automated processes support rapid business growth. Future-Proofing and Technology Evolution ISMS.online Focus : Gradual evolution of existing comprehensive platform. Emphasis on stability and comprehensive functionality. Adoptech Focus : Continuous innovation in automation and AI capabilities. Investment in emerging compliance requirements (like CAF, NIS2, DORA). Adoptech’s forward-looking approach includes AI-enhanced compliance automation and an expanding integration ecosystem. Return on Investment Analysis ISMS.online ROI Considerations Investment Requirements : Higher initial platform costs (£15,000-£50,000+ annually) and significant implementation resource allocation. Break-Even Analysis : Typically 12-18 months for large organisations. Adoptech ROI Considerations Investment Requirements : Lower platform costs (starting at £250/month) and minimal implementation resource requirements. Reduced ongoing administrative overhead through automation. Break-Even Analysis : Typically 3-6 months due to lower costs and immediate efficiency gains. Analysis: For a typical 100-employee organisation over three years, Adoptech provides superior financial value. Conclusion: Making Your Platform Decision Both ISMS.online and Adoptech serve important roles, but they target different organisational needs and resource constraints. ISMS.online represents the comprehensive, traditional approach. It serves large enterprises with dedicated compliance teams who require extensive customisation and detailed manual control over compliance processes. Its strength lies in its comprehensive feature set and ability to handle complex, unique requirements. Adoptech embodies the modern, efficient approach. It serves organisations of all sizes seeking to achieve enterprise-grade compliance without the associated complexity and resource requirements. Its strength lies in its intelligent automation, expert support, and ability to deliver rapid value with minimal resource investment. The Strategic Recommendation For most organisations evaluating compliance platforms in 2025, Adoptech provides superior value through its combination of automation, expert support, pricing structure, and rapid implementation. The platform’s modern approach aligns with modern business needs for efficiency, scalability, and cost-effectiveness while maintaining the thoroughness required for successful compliance outcomes. We recommend Adoptech for any organisation seeking to reduce administrative burden, achieve rapid time-to-value, manage multiple frameworks efficiently, and benefit from expert UK-based guidance, especially those with limited dedicated compliance resources or a focus on budget optimisation. ISMS.online remains a viable option for the largest enterprises that have significant dedicated compliance teams (5 or more FTE compliance professionals) and substantial budget flexibility. It is best suited for scenarios requiring extensive customisation and detailed manual control over compliance processes, typically in highly complex or uniquely regulated industries. However, for the majority of businesses, ISMS.online’s higher cost and reliance on manual processes and internal resources make it a less efficient choice compared to Adoptech’s automated, value-driven solution. Next Steps for Decision Making Assessment Phase : Evaluate your organisation’s resource availability, technical requirements, and budget constraints. Demonstration Phase : Request detailed demonstrations from both platforms focusing on your specific use cases. Pilot Consideration : Consider starting with a limited scope pilot to evaluate real-world performance. The compliance platform decision will influence your organisation’s security posture, operational efficiency, and certification success for years to come. Choose the platform that best aligns with your organisational capacity, growth trajectory, and strategic objectives. Ready to experience the Adoptech advantage? Contact our team for a personalised demonstration and see how intelligent automation can transform your compliance approach while delivering the expert support you need for guaranteed certification success. About This Analysis: This comparison is based on publicly available information, user reviews, and official platform documentation as of November 2025. Pricing and features may vary based on specific requirements and negotiations. Always request current quotes and demos for the most accurate information Al Goodwin Back to News

section: Adoptech vs Vanta vs Drata: The Complete Compliance Platform Comparison
text: TLDR : Choosing the right compliance automation platform is critical for every business. This comprehensive comparison breaks down Adoptech vs Vanta vs Drata across features, pricing, integrations, and suitability for different business sizes. For UK and European businesses, Adoptech stands out as the most cost-effective, supportive, and reliable compliance platform, with a 100% certification pass rate. While Vanta and Drata have strengths for specific niches, Adoptech is the smarter long-term choice for most SMEs within regulated industries. We detail below the pros/cons and insights you need to make an informed decision. Introduction: The Current Security Compliance Platform Landscape Security compliance has become a business enabler rather than just a regulatory checkbox. With data breaches costing organizations an average of $4.45 million per incident, and non-compliance fines reaching €20 million or 4% of annual global turnover, choosing the right compliance automation platform has never been more important. Three platforms consistently emerge at the forefront of security compliance automation discussions: Adoptech , Vanta , and Drata . Each offers unique strengths, but understanding their differences helps businesses select the right fit for their compliance journey. Platform Overview: Understanding Each Solution Adoptech: UK-Based Expertise Meets Intelligent ISMS Automation Adoptech is an intelligent platform to connect, manage, and automate all security compliance processes, certifications, audits and tests across multiple frameworks. Its UK-based roots ensure deep knowledge of European compliance requirements. Combining platform automation with dedicated consultancy support, Adoptech provides more than software, it delivers a true outsourced security compliance service. Key differentiator : a proven 100% certification pass rate for internally audited clients, unmatched in the industry. Vanta: Startup-Friendly Trust Management Founded in 2018 in San Francisco, Vanta is popular with early-stage SaaS startups. It focuses on trust management and continuous monitoring, with strong brand presence and extensive integrations. However, it is often costly, less suited for regulated industries, and charges extra for premium support. Key differentiator: AI agents and automated workflows handle the manual tasks and identify gaps in your programme. Drata: AI-Native Automation for Scaling Enterprises Founded in 2020 in San Diego, Drata positions itself as an AI-native compliance platform. It offers enterprise-grade risk management and deep automation, and is better suited to large US-based enterprises than SMEs or European businesses. Key differentiator: Advanced customisation and enterprise-grade risk management. Feature Comparison Matrix Supported Compliance Frameworks Platform Frameworks Supported Key Frameworks Adoptech 10+ frameworks ISO 27001, SOC 2, GDPR, DORA, ISO 9001, Cyber Essentials Vanta 35+ frameworks SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, PCI, and GDPR Drata 20+ frameworks SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, and more Verdict : Vanta covers the most frameworks, but Adoptech focuses on the ones UK/EU businesses, without charging for extras. Automation Capabilities Platform Automation Level Key Features Adoptech 80-90% automation Automate 90% of tasks and processes, connect vendors, teams and tools across your organisation Vanta 70-85% automation AI agents and automated workflows handle the manual tasks Drata 70-90% automation AI-powered automation, deep integrations, and a unified platform Verdict : Adoptech leads with 90% automation, striking the right balance between efficiency and expert oversight. Integration Ecosystem Platform Integration Count Notable Integrations Adoptech 50+ integrations Cloud providers, identity providers, task management, security apps Vanta 250+ integrations broad integration coverage (250–375+ compliance tools) Drata 120+ integrations integrates with over 200 applications and systems Verdict : Vanta provides the most comprehensive integration ecosystem, while Adoptech provides a curated set of essential integrations, avoiding the ‘integration bloat’ that can slow down implementations. User Experience & Interface Platform Strengths User Feedback Adoptech Straightforward, accessible interface Users highlight ease of use and minimal learning curve Vanta fast deployment, broad integration coverage Praised for intuitive setup and startup-friendly design Drata clean layout, real-time dashboards, and polished design Technical teams appreciate sophisticated dashboard capabilities Verdict : Adoptech and Vanta for come out top for ease of use, while Drata may be best for advanced users requiring detailed analytics. Pricing Analysis: Understanding Your Investment Even at enterprise scale, Adoptech delivers lower total cost of ownership, because expert consultancy support is included at every tier, avoiding the hidden add-ons and premium support fees charged by competitors. This makes Adoptech not only the most affordable entry point but also the best value at enterprise scale. By contrast, Vanta typically ranges from $10,000 to $80,000+ annually, with add-on costs for frameworks and premium support. Drata starts at $7,500 annually, with enterprise plans often exceeding $100,000. While both provide strong features, their pricing is prohibitive for many SMEs and European organisations. Adoptech Pricing Structure Pricing Tiers : Starter : £64/month (~$768/year) Professional : Custom pricing Enterprise : Custom pricing with full consultancy services Value Proposition : Most affordable entry point and vest value at enterprise scale, with included UK-based expert support at every tier. Vanta Pricing Structure Vanta’s pricing can go from $10,000 to up to $80,000 per year, depending on the number of devices, frameworks, and features selected. Pricing Tiers : Essential : ~$10,000/year (small businesses, basic frameworks) Pro : $15,000-$30,000/year (growing companies) Scale : $30,000-$50,000/year (expanding businesses) Enterprise : $50,000-$80,000+/year (large organizations) Key Insights : additional frameworks can be added for $5K (and are often discounted if you have multiple add-ons) Drata Pricing Structure Drata’s pricing starts at $7500/year, which is called the essential plan, with enterprise plans reaching $100,000+. Pricing Tiers : Foundation : ~$7,500-$40,000/year (actual cost $14,000-$23,000 after discounts) Advanced : ~$37,000/year (actual cost $13,600-$21,700 after discounts) Enterprise : $50,000-$100,000+/year Value Insight : typical discounts range from 42% (median) to 64% (95th percentile) Pricing Comparison Summary Platform Entry Level Mid-Market Enterprise Best For Adoptech $768/year Custom Custom Cost-conscious startups, UK/EU companies. Vanta $10,000/year $15-30k/year $50-80k/year Fast-growing SaaS companies Drata $7,500/year $15-25k/year $50-100k+/year Scaling enterprises Verdict : Adoptech offers not only the most affordable entry point for small companies, but also the best value at enterprise level scale. Target Market Analysis Adoptech: Adoptech suits not only startups and SMEs but also larger organisations in regulated sectors that need reliability without excessive spend. Ideal For UK and European businesses requiring local compliance expertise Startups and SMEs with limited budgets seeking expert guidance Regulated industries needing specialized frameworks like DORA Organizations wanting a balance of automation and human support Key Advantages : 100% certification pass rate UK-based expert support included Most affordable entry pricing Strong focus on European compliance requirements Vanta: Strong for startups, but less effective for companies beyond early stage. Ideal For Early-stage SaaS startups pursuing first-time compliance Fast-growing companies needing quick deployment Organizations requiring extensive third-party integrations Teams prioritizing ease of use over advanced features Key Advantages : Largest integration ecosystem (250+ tools) fast deployment Strong brand recognition and market presence AI-powered automation capabilities Drata: Designed for US enterprises; rarely cost-effective for UK/EU SMEs. Ideal For Medium to large enterprises with complex compliance needs Organizations managing multiple frameworks simultaneously Companies requiring advanced risk and vendor management Technical teams wanting deep customization capabilities Key Advantages : advanced customization, strong risk and vendor management tools AI-native platform with sophisticated automation Excellent for audit collaboration and enterprise workflows Strong customer support reputation Strengths & Weaknesses Analysis Adoptech Strengths : Lowest cost entry point in the market Easy to use, accessible interface Dedicated UK-based compliance experts Streamlined integrations to reduce complexity and tool bloat. 100% certification success rate Strong focus on European regulations (GDPR, DORA) Includes policy generation and document management Weaknesses : Limited global presence outside UK/Europe Less established brand recognition Fewer advanced automation features for enterprise needs Vanta Strengths : Comprehensive integration library (250+ tools) Excellent user experience and quick setup Strong AI-powered automation Broad framework support (35+) Well-established market presence Weaknesses : Higher pricing than alternatives Statuses didn’t work for us — we never knew what was ‘done’ Limited customization for complex environments Can be overwhelming for very small organizations Drata Strengths : Advanced automation and AI capabilities Strong enterprise-grade features Excellent customer support Deep customization options Competitive pricing with significant discounts available Weaknesses : The platform’s rigidity may constrict teams with unique regulatory needs Steeper learning curve for non-technical users Fewer integrations than Vanta Limited transparency in public pricing Implementation & Support Comparison Onboarding Experience Platform Onboarding Time Support Level Success Rate Adoptech 2-4 weeks High-touch with dedicated specialists 100% certification pass rate Vanta 1-2 weeks Self-service with guided setup Industry standard Drata 2-6 weeks Professional services available High success rate Customer Support Adoptech : Personal relationship with UK-based compliance experts, included in all plans Unlike competitors who charge extra for premium support, Adoptech includes direct access to compliance specialists in every plan, making onboarding faster and certification likely Vanta : Tiered support model with premium support available as an add-on. Drata : Strong customer support department and more consistent feature updates than Vanta. ROI & Business Impact Analysis Time to Compliance Platform Average SOC 2 Timeline Effort Reduction Cost Savings Adoptech 3-4 months 80-90% Significant (due to lower pricing) Vanta 3-5 months 70-85% Moderate Drata 3-6 months 70-90% Reduced our overall cost in engineering and auditing by at least 60% Long-term Value Adoptech : With lower upfront and ongoing costs, combined with included expert guidance, Adoptech consistently delivers the highest ROI for compliance teams. Vanta : Ideal for companies prioritizing speed to market and integration flexibility. Drata : Superior for large enterprises requiring comprehensive risk management and multiple frameworks. Making Your Decision: Selection Framework Choose Adoptech If: Budget is a primary concern (under £10k annually) You’re a UK/European business needing local expertise You prefer human guidance alongside automation You need DORA or other EU-specific framework support You’re a startup or SME with basic compliance needs You’re a larger organisation in a regulated sector that requires an ‘all in one’ platform, with support included. Choose Vanta If: You need extensive third-party integrations (250+ tools) Speed to market is critical You’re an early-stage SaaS company User experience and ease of use are top priorities You’re looking for software only You have budget flexibility ($15k+ annually) Choose Drata If: You manage multiple complex compliance frameworks Advanced customization and risk management are essential You’re a medium to large enterprise Technical sophistication is important to your team You need enterprise-grade audit collaboration features Future-Proofing Your Compliance Strategy Technology Trends All three platforms are investing heavily in AI automation, but with different approaches: Adoptech : AI-enhanced human expertise Vanta : AI agents and automated workflows Drata : AI-native platform with deep learning capabilities Market Evolution The compliance automation market is rapidly evolving toward: Greater AI integration and automation Real-time continuous monitoring Enhanced vendor risk management Integrated security training and awareness Conclusion: The Verdict There’s no universal “best” platform—the right choice depends entirely on your specific needs, budget, and organizational context. While Vanta and Drata may suit narrow use cases, Adoptech delivers the strongest combination of affordability, expert support, and proven certification success. For most UK and European businesses, it is the clear choice. For Budget-Conscious UK/EU Businesses : Adoptech delivers exceptional value with expert support at the lowest cost point. For Fast-Growing SaaS Startups : Vanta’s extensive integrations and user-friendly approach make it the clear choice for rapid deployment. For Enterprise Organizations : Drata’s sophisticated automation and customization capabilities provide the scalability and control needed for complex compliance environments. Next Steps Assess Your Requirements : Framework needs, team size, technical sophistication, budget Request Demos : All three platforms offer personalized demonstrations Consider Implementation Timeline : Factor in onboarding time and resource availability Evaluate Total Cost of Ownership : Include platform costs, implementation, and ongoing support Start Small : Consider beginning with core frameworks and expanding over time The compliance platform you choose today will shape your security posture and business growth for years to come. Take the time to evaluate each option thoroughly, considering not just current needs but future requirements as your organization scales. Ready to transform your compliance approach? Contact the platform that best matches your requirements for a personalized demonstration and pricing discussion. About This Analysis : This comparison is based on publicly available information, user reviews, and official platform documentation as of September 2025. Pricing and features may vary based on specific requirements and negotiations. Always request current quotes and demos for the most accurate information. Al Goodwin Back to News

meta_description: Simplifying InfoSec Certification. Adoptech provides the most simple and cost-effective way to achieve and maintain Cyber Essentials, ISO 27001 and SOC 2.

section: What are the benefits of hiring an ISO 27001 Consultant?
text: Implementing any ISO certification is a strategic move enabling companies to win and retain business through an internationally recognised standard. Historically ISO certifications require a very high level of involvement and time but Adoptech has simplified this process and made achieving certification faster and more cost effective. ISO 27001 is recognised as the “best practice” standard for Information Security, and requires the implementation and maintenance of an Information Security Management System ( ISMS ) to protect the company’s and clients’ information in a systematic and cost-effective way. W hat that means in practice is identifying and managing information security risks by implementing controls. The current standard is in the process of being updated but at present 114 controls need to be assessed to determine whether they are applicable. Overseeing all these controls can be time consuming and particularly onerous for smaller companies. Some growing companies hire a CISO who understands the processes and procedures that need to be implemented in order to fulfill the certifications requirements. But for the majority of SMEs, hiring a CISO is not an option and external guidance is required . The standard and its implementation is simple but like most things in life, its only simple if you know how . With or without a CISO, a company can benefit from the time saved using the Adoptech platform and also having Adoptech SMEs support their internal audits and conducting dress-rehearsals for external audits. At Adoptech our ISO SMEs are known as practitioners ,  we deliberately make the distinction between a consultant and a practitioner because our team is actively engaged rather than simply consulting. How can an Adoptech ISO practitioner help your business achieve ISO 27001 Certification? : Expertise Adoptech Practitioners are subject matter experts , with years of experience in the certification process and detailed understanding of the processes and procedures to ensure the fastest and most efficient certification possible for different types of companies. Their job is not only to ensure certification, but also to ensure that the entire team understands the processes for ongoing maintenance of the certification. Efficiency The main objective of an Adoptech practitioner is to help you achieve certification as fast and cost effectively as possible ; they will oversee the process and ensure that momentum is maintained whilst ensuring you have an efficient ISMS (information security management  system) in place that achieves the objective of keeping your company and your clients’ data secure. Someone who knows the ISO standards can help you implement changes to achieve certification more quickly; you spend less time trying to interpret the standards and more time complying with them. Ultimately less time away from day to day activities means the company’s business will continue as usual during the certification process. Bespoke solution Each company has its own processes, employees, founders, teams, workflows, platforms… this makes each certification process unique . Adoptech’s practitioners have vast experience of implementing Information Security Management Systems (ISMS’s) in many different companies. They use the Adoptech portal which offers a simple starting point, and tailor it to your company. Mid and Long term solution Achieving certification requires a stage 1 and stage 2 external audit to be passed. Preparing for this takes most SMEs between 3 and 6 months. The stage 1 and 2 audits typically take 3-5 days for an SME to complete. After certification following a 3 year cycle, once certification has been achieved annual surveillance audits are undertaken and in year 3 the full stage 1 and 2 audit are required. An Adoptech practitioner will help you ensure the workflows and processes implemented minimise the efforts to maintain the certification and avoid having to re implement each control on an annual basis. In summary, the ISO 27001 consultant’s job is to make the certification process as efficient and cost effective as possible for the company. They will also ensure that the entire team understands the processes for ongoing maintenance of the certification and hav e the necessary knowledge to participate in the recertification process. When choosing an ISO 27001 consultant, it is important to choose someone with broad experience in ISO certification and a proven track record of helping companies successfully achieve certification. At Adoptech , we are simplifying Information Security Certification, with our team of experts providing dedicated support to help you prepare for, achieve and maintain your certification. We’ve done the work to make certification simple and cost effective. Contact us if you want to know how we can help you achieve your ISO 27001 certification, or read our ISO 27001 guide if you need more information. Talk to an expert Al Goodwin Back to News

section: Best Vanta Alternatives: Compare Top Compliance Software
text: Top Vanta Alternatives: Finding the Best Compliance Software Finding the right compliance automation solution can be challenging, given the many factors that must be considered: SOC 2 compliance requirements , vendor management complexity, budget constraints, and the specific security and compliance needs of your organisation. The best way to select a solution is by evaluating multiple Vanta competitors to compare their features and approach. While automation is a crucial component of compliance, the right compliance SaaS solution should align with your organisation’s long-term security goals, not just provide a checkbox exercise. Vanta: Features & Offerings Vanta, a San Francisco-based cybersecurity platform , specialises in security monitoring, SOC 2 compliance automation , and risk assessment. It helps businesses achieve compliance with frameworks such as ISO 27001 compliance requirements , SOC 2, and HIPAA. Key Features of Vanta Automated compliance risk software monitoring and reporting Third-party risk management solutions (via TrustPage acquisition) Pre-built security questionnaires and documentation management Vanta Trust Reports for demonstrating security posture While Vanta provides an automated path to compliance, it is important to consider whether a one-size-fits-all approach meets your specific business needs. Best Vanta Alternatives: Top Competitors Adoptech: Human-Led, Tech-Empowered Compliance Adoptech is a UK-based compliance software provider offering ISO 27001 compliance software and SOC 2 compliance automation tools. While many VC-backed rivals chase rapid growth, Adoptech takes a different approach—prioritising lasting partnerships and placing information security at the core, with compliance and certifications as natural outcomes. Why Choose Adoptech? ✅ Personalised, expert-led service – compliance specialists work directly with your team ✅ UK & EU specialists – ensuring alignment with ISO 27001 certification requirements and DORA ✅ AI-powered policy wizard – generating 80+ tailored security policies ✅ Expertise in regulated industries – including compliance software for financial services ✅ Bespoke automation & integration – reducing inefficiencies Potential Considerations ✖ Fewer off-the-shelf integrations than larger competitors ✖ No built-in asset discovery (requires third-party risk management tools ) ✖ Limited third-party risk management software functionality Adoptech vs. Vanta: Key Differences Hands-on consultancy vs. fully automated platform UK & EU compliance expertise vs. global generalist approach Tailored security policies vs. pre-mapped frameworks Long-term client relationships vs. compliance software comparison based on automation alone For businesses needing hands-on support, Adoptech’s consultative approach ensures compliance is not just achieved but embedded into operations . Drata: Security & Compliance Automation Drata is a compliance software provider that automates SOC 2 compliance monitoring. Pros ✔ User-friendly interface ✔ Strong customer support ✔ Comprehensive policy builder ✔ Beginner-friendly onboarding Cons ✖ Limited integration options ✖ No asset discovery Drata vs. Vanta Vanta vs. Drata : Vanta offers 21 compliance frameworks, while Drata provides 14 Drata is praised for customer support but has fewer integrations Secureframe: Compliance & Security Automation Secureframe offers ISO 27001 internal audit requirements monitoring but has received criticism for usability issues. Pros ✔ Strong integrations ✔ Clear ISO 27001 compliance requirements checklists ✔ Good customer support Cons ✖ Poor user experience ✖ Complaints about onboarding ✖ Weak third-party risk management software capabilities Secureframe vs. Vanta Secureframe vs. Vanta : Secureframe offers detailed checklists, but Vanta is more reliable due to fewer reported issues. UpGuard: Third-Party Risk Management & Security Ratings UpGuard focuses on third-party risk management tools and enterprise risk management software rather than compliance automation. Pros ✔ Vendor risk scoring ✔ Security ratings & risk management software ✔ Executive security reports Cons ✖ No automation for security certifications ✖ Smaller shared assessment library UpGuard vs. Vanta UpGuard is stronger in third-party risk management software , while Vanta excels in compliance automation . Conclusion: Choosing the Right Compliance Software Security compliance is not just about ticking boxes—it’s about embedding best practices into your business. While Vanta competitors like Drata, Secureframe, and UpGuard offer automation, they may not provide the tailored support needed for regulated industries. Adoptech stands out by balancing automation with expert consultancy , ensuring compliance aligns with ISO 27001 certification requirements , SOC 2, and evolving regulations. For businesses seeking a compliance software UK provider with a focus on hands-on support, Adoptech is the clear choice . Al Goodwin Back to News

text: What policies should a company have in the UK? If you are getting your business started in the UK, you may need some guidance on the policies that are required, so let’s start with our quick guide on the company policies required in the UK. When you create a company policy, you efficiently communicate the rules, expectations, and plans of an organisation to staff, customers and other interested parties. By providing clarity and the context of why processes are important to your company, customers and wider community, you will create a more productive and positive workplace. A company’s culture and the expected standards of behaviour aren’t immediately clear to everyone which can lead to problems in the workplace. The behaviour of existing staff, especially senior management is often the most compelling way to demonstrate what is expected but company policies are also a useful tool for removing any ambiguity as they provide clear direction on how staff are expected to behave. Poorly worded company policies can lead to misunderstanding, confusion and conflict. Adoptech has made it easy to create a clearly worded policy; you are provided with guidance on best practices as you create your company policies, but if you aren’t sure, you can always chat with one of the team. Who is interested in your policies? Company policies are not just of interest to your staff. External stakeholders such as  prospective customers, customers and investors, will all be interested in viewing your company policies since they provide a clear guide on the governance and culture of your company. Adoptech has made sharing policies easy; at the click of a button, you can share a link to an individual or multiple company policies, avoiding time wasted with emailing stakeholders each time a policy is updated. Take a look at our library with over 70 policies here . Do I really need all these company policies? The number and type of policies required depends entirely upon your industry, geographical location, workplace culture and size of your company. The country or countries within which you operate are likely to require some policies to be in place, for example in the UK at a minimum you are likely to require: Health and Safety Policy Grievance Policy Equal Opportunities and Diversity Policy Maternity/Paternity Policy Data Protection Policy Website Privacy Policy If you sell software in regulated sectors such as financial services you are very likely to need a large number of clearly documented policies in place and be in a position to evidence that those policies are being upheld in order to successfully sell services in the sector. Company policies may also be needed for the legal protection of your company, if this is the case we always advise that you take legal advice when creating your policies to ensure they provide the specific protection you require. Be proactive rather than reactive We often see examples of companies implementing policies after an event, for example, after a staff dispute has commenced, a policy is rushed out to outline expected behaviour or, after an information security incident has occured, a password policy is introduced. How to write a company policy? When drafting a policy, always keep in mind that the objective is to clearly communicate and inform the reader about your company. Ensure that it’s as unique as your company. Every company is different and your company policies should reflect that. Keep it short Short and simple is the most effective way to communicate. This will make the contents more memorable and help prevent your staff from feeling patronised. Traditional all-encompassing information security policies and staff handbooks are a challenge to read and rarely remembered. Avoid ambiguity Simple, clear and plain language should be used to make it clear what guidelines are coming into effect. Use persuasive language It is more common now to use positive, persuasive language for SaaS company policies, such as ”should”, rather than dictative terms such as “must”, this is because you are more likely to receive a positive response from your staff. Collaborate Every company policy should be drafted in conjunction with the senior management team and approved by them to ensure it reflects their view before being finalised. Policies are typically approved by the Board of Directors in SaaS companies. Adoptech’s built-in approval flow makes this often iterative and cumbersome process seamless. Maintain a clean design Limit the use of colour on your document headers or logos to maintain a professional appearance. Writing should always be in black or dark gray against a white background so that it can be easily read. Try to use white space and separate the policy into easily understandable clauses. Use a formal font to help convey that the policy should be taken seriously. Communicate the policies Once a policy has been created, it then needs to be communicated to staff and you need to ensure they have read and understood it. The Adoptech platform enables policies to be shared with staff using a deadline for them to read and attest in a seamless process. An audit trail of their attestation is maintained in the platform enabling you to evidence good governance. Queries and Follow Ups Setting up all these policies and ensuring they reflect the specific needs of your company can be time-consuming and overwhelming when you are focused on growing your business. Adoptech has made it easy, chat with a member of the team for more information or just visit our policy generator to find out about our library with over 70 company policies. Al Goodwin Back to News

section: What policies should a company have in the UK?
text: If you are getting your business started in the UK, you may need some guidance on the policies that are required, so let’s start with our quick guide on the company policies required in the UK. When you create a company policy, you efficiently communicate the rules, expectations, and plans of an organisation to staff, customers and other interested parties. By providing clarity and the context of why processes are important to your company, customers and wider community, you will create a more productive and positive workplace. A company’s culture and the expected standards of behaviour aren’t immediately clear to everyone which can lead to problems in the workplace. The behaviour of existing staff, especially senior management is often the most compelling way to demonstrate what is expected but company policies are also a useful tool for removing any ambiguity as they provide clear direction on how staff are expected to behave. Poorly worded company policies can lead to misunderstanding, confusion and conflict. Adoptech has made it easy to create a clearly worded policy; you are provided with guidance on best practices as you create your company policies, but if you aren’t sure, you can always chat with one of the team.
